extra.reason: probe — bd write path was broken after v1.0.2 migration; fixed by setting issue_prefix in config

Random LAA MAC generated per VmConfig at build() time, persisted on the VmConfig so save/restore round-trips. Replaces the hardcoded 76:c4:f2:a0:00:01 literal. Verified: (1) 4 unit tests in config_test.rs (LAA bit set, distinct MACs across configs, explicit .mac() preserved, Clone shares MAC); (2) runtime_e2e smoke_pull_and_run_alpine passed end-to-end with the new MAC path; (3) stack_e2e complex_stack_snapshot_restore_rewinds_shared_vm_state passed end-to-end (save/restore correctness preserved).

VsockListener::accept() now returns AcceptedVsockStream { stream, source: VmHandle } instead of bare VsockStream. VmHandle is a Copy/Eq/Hash/Send/Sync u64 derived from the source VZVirtioSocketDevice pointer that the framework delivers on every accept callback (was previously dropped as _device at vsock.rs:322). Verified: (1) 2 vsock_test unit tests confirm trait derivations; (2) workspace build clean; (3) runtime_e2e smoke_pull_and_run_alpine passed end-to-end. No external callers updated — only vz internal code consumes accept(). VmHandle is exposed at crate root via vz::VmHandle.

Replaced VmConfig.disk_path: Option<PathBuf> + dead disk_size_bytes with disks: Vec<DiskConfig>. New DiskConfig{id,path,read_only} and .disk(DiskConfig) builder method (cut over per project policy — no shim). Bridge iterates the Vec and constructs one VZVirtioBlockDeviceConfiguration per entry, honoring read_only. Updated all in-tree callers: vz-linux/src/config.rs, vz-sandbox/src/pool.rs, vz-cli/src/commands/run.rs, vz/tests/bridge_test.rs, vz/tests/config_test.rs, lib.rs doctest. macOS boot validation now requires non-empty disks; Linux can boot with empty disks (initramfs only). Added VmConfig::disks() accessor. Verified: (1) 3 new unit tests for multi-disk ordering/preservation, no-disk Linux boot, macOS-without-disks rejection; (2) full vz crate test suite (68 tests) green; (3) downstream vz-linux + vz-sandbox unit tests (78 tests) green; (4) cargo clippy -p vz --lib clean; (5) runtime_e2e smoke_pull_and_run_alpine passed (single-disk path through new Vec); (6) stack_e2e snapshot test passed end-to-end. Multi-disk-specific E2E (rootfs + data + mount/write/save/restore) deferred — requires custom Linux kernel cmdline + multi-mount initramfs not currently in test infra; unit ordering tests + framework array contract cover the gap.

Wired up VZVirtioTraditionalMemoryBalloonDeviceConfiguration. Builder gains .memory_balloon(bool) (default ENABLED — strictly additive, opt-out for consumers who want pure-VM behavior). Bridge attaches the balloon via setMemoryBalloonDevices when enabled. Vm gains target_memory_size() / set_target_memory_size(bytes) async methods that walk memoryBalloonDevices, downcast to VZVirtioTraditionalMemoryBalloonDevice, and read/write Apple's targetVirtualMachineMemorySize property. When balloon is disabled, both methods return VzError::InvalidConfig. Verified: (1) 2 unit tests in config_test.rs (default-on, opt-out); (2) workspace builds clean; (3) runtime_e2e smoke_pull_and_run_alpine passed (VM boots fine with balloon attached); (4) NEW balloon_test.rs runtime E2E (signed, run via codesign + entitlement) — both tests passed in 0.42s: roundtrip reads configured memory initially, accepts and stores target/2 (rounded down to 1 MB), and verifies disabled-balloon path returns the expected error. Functional reclaim-after-dirty validation deferred to vz-o4j (the dependent bead this unblocks).

Measurement complete. Headline: lazy paging works as designed. 10 idle VMs configured at 1 GB each cost the host 2.28 GB (22.3% of configured), not the naive 10 GB.

PER-VM RSS COST AT IDLE (size sweep, N=5):
  Configured/VM | RSS/VM | Ratio
  ---------------+---------+-------
  512 MB        | 217 MB  | 42.5%
  1024 MB       | 228 MB  | 22.3%
  2048 MB       | 250 MB  | 12.2%
  4096 MB       | 354 MB  | 8.6%

Per-VM cost grows by only ~140 MB when configured grows from 512 MB to 4 GB — overwhelmingly framework + kernel image + initramfs + page tables, not declared guest memory. Apple ships each VM in its own com.apple.Virtualization.VirtualMachine.xpc service (PPID=1 launchd-managed), and the guest's physical memory lives in that helper's RSS as anonymous lazy pages. The test process itself stays at ~17 MB regardless of N.

Methodology: walks ps -axo for the XPC helper comm, sums RSS delta vs pre-test baseline. Three test cases in crates/vz/tests/memory_footprint_test.rs.

BALLOON RECLAIM: NOT functional on currently-shipped kernel. set_target_memory_size returned 0 MB to host even with hugepages=256 cmdline. Root cause: linux/vz-linux.config:109 has CONFIG_VIRTIO_BALLOON=n, so the guest has no balloon driver to respond to inflate requests. The vz-jhr host API path is correct (verified separately in balloon_test.rs against Apple's framework); the gap is purely guest-side. Filed vz-7l6 to enable the kernel option and rerun.

Set CONFIG_VIRTIO_BALLOON=y in linux/vz-linux.config; rebuilt kernel via 'make docker-build' (new vmlinux SHA256: 1c000dc25846cbbd7fbca71cade067bac00bc6e9f9eb979d6ca04434cf9e8369). Verified end-to-end with three new gated tests:

(1) balloon_kernel_probe_test.rs: guest virtio bus shows /sys/bus/virtio/devices/virtio1 with device=0x0005 bound to driver=virtio_balloon. Driver IS loaded.

(2) balloon_inflate_test.rs: set_target_memory_size(512MB) on a 2GB VM caused guest MemAvailable to drop from 1,946 MB to 381 MB — a 1,565 MB drop. The balloon driver IS responding to Apple's request and inflating inside the guest.

(3) memory_footprint_test::balloon_reclaims_real_pages_under_load with t=5/15/30/60s sampling: host XPC helper RSS stayed within +6 MB of pre-balloon baseline. Apple appears to keep the balloon-marked pages mapped in the helper process — they don't return to macOS via 'ps -o rss=' until memory pressure forces it.

Net: API path works, guest driver works, but observable host RSS reclaim from balloon does NOT happen at idle. This is consistent with Apple's framework treating balloon as a 'soft hint' — macOS can take those pages when needed, but holds them until then. For idle-VM resource accounting on a non-pressured host, lazy paging (vz-o4j data) is the headline mechanism, NOT ballooning. Balloon only matters under memory pressure or for over-commit scenarios where macOS will actually pull on the helper.

Also added initramfs cmdline knobs: 'memhog_mb=N' (preallocate N MB in tmpfs at boot, for testing dirty-memory paths) and 'vz_idle=1' (skip guest agent + youki, just sleep with periodic /proc/meminfo dumps to serial). Both are no-op when their cmdline arg is absent.
